title: Sprint #11 Planning
description:
## Sprint #11 Planning

### Focus Areas

This sprint marks the beginning of a new development cycle for our AI Dev Team Simulation project. The team should focus on:

- Identifying high-value features that enhance the simulation experience
- Improving the realism and interactivity of the agent workflow visualization
- Addressing any technical debt or architectural improvements
- Considering user experience enhancements

### Call for Proposals

**Carlos (Senior Developer)** and **Ana (Junior Developer)** - please propose features for this sprint:

- **Carlos**: Consider architectural improvements, complex features, or technical infrastructure tasks that will benefit the project long-term
- **Ana**: Focus on UI components, user-facing features, or enhancements to existing interfaces

### Guidelines

- Proposed tasks should be achievable within a single sprint
- Tasks should not create file conflicts between developers
- Each developer should aim for 1-2 substantial tasks
- Consider the existing tech stack (React 19, TypeScript, TanStack ecosystem)

Please add your proposals as comments to this issue.

comments.author: Sprint Orchestrator
comments.body:
## Sprint #11 Tasks Created

Based on Carlos's proposals, the following tasks have been created:

| Issue | Title | Assignee | Complexity | Branch |
|-------|-------|----------|------------|--------|
| FAB-12 | Sprint Management Dashboard - Backend & State | Carlos | Senior | feature/sprint-management-backend |
| FAB-13 | Sprint Management Dashboard - UI & Forms | Ana | Junior | feature/sprint-management-ui |
| FAB-14 | Task Assignment & Queue System | Carlos | Senior | feature/task-assignment-system |
| FAB-15 | Agent Performance Analytics Dashboard | Ana | Junior | feature/agent-analytics-ui |

**Architecture approach:**
- Carlos handles data layer, routing setup, MSW handlers, and TanStack Query hooks
- Ana builds UI components, forms, and visualization layers
- This split minimizes file conflicts while allowing parallel work

All tasks are now ready for implementation. Sprint #11 begins!

title: Sprint Management Dashboard - Backend & State
description:
## Overview
Implement the backend infrastructure and state management for the Sprint Management Dashboard feature.

## Scope
- Create MSW handlers for sprint CRUD operations:
  - `GET /api/sprints` - list all sprints
  - `POST /api/sprints` - create new sprint
  - `GET /api/sprints/:id` - get sprint details
  - `PATCH /api/sprints/:id` - update sprint (name, description, dates, status)
- Implement TanStack Query hooks:
  - `useSprints()` - fetch sprint list
  - `useSprintDetails(sprintId)` - fetch single sprint
  - `useCreateSprint()` - mutation for creating sprints
  - `useUpdateSprint()` - mutation for updating sprints
- Create TypeScript types for sprint data:
  - `Sprint` interface with id, name, description, startDate, endDate, goal, status
  - `SprintStatus` enum: `PLANNING | ACTIVE | COMPLETED`
- Set up routing structure:
  - `/sprints` - sprint list route
  - `/sprints/:sprintId` - sprint detail route
- Create mock data factory for realistic sprint data in MSW

## Technical Notes
- Follow existing patterns in `src/mocks/handlers.ts`
- Use TanStack Query for caching and optimistic updates
- Ensure type safety across all endpoints
- Sprint state machine: PLANNING → ACTIVE → COMPLETED (enforce in handlers)

## Files to Create/Modify
- `src/mocks/handlers.ts` - add sprint endpoints
- `src/mocks/data/sprints.ts` - mock sprint data factory
- `src/hooks/useSprints.ts` - sprint query hooks
- `src/types/sprint.ts` - sprint type definitions
- `src/routes/__root.tsx` or router config - add sprint routes

title: Sprint Management Dashboard - UI & Forms
description:
## Overview
Build the user interface and form components for the Sprint Management Dashboard.

## Scope
- Create sprint list page component (`/sprints`):
  - Display all sprints in a card grid or list layout
  - Show sprint name, dates, status badge, and progress indicator
  - "Create Sprint" button
  - Filter/sort controls (by status, date)
- Build sprint creation/editing form using TanStack Form:
  - Form fields: name (text), description (textarea), start date, end date
  - Goal editor with markdown support (simple textarea for MVP)
  - Form validation: required fields, date range validation
  - Submit/cancel buttons with loading states
- Create sprint detail page component (`/sprints/:sprintId`):
  - Display sprint header with name, dates, status
  - Show goal/description in formatted view
  - Edit button to open form in edit mode
  - Status transition buttons (e.g., "Start Sprint", "Complete Sprint")
- Update sidebar to show active sprint context
  - Display active sprint name/dates in sidebar
  - Link to active sprint detail page
- Create reusable components:
  - `SprintCard` - sprint summary card
  - `SprintStatusBadge` - colored status indicator
  - `SprintForm` - form component using TanStack Form

## Technical Notes
- Use TanStack Form for form state management and validation
- Follow existing component patterns and styling conventions
- Integrate with hooks from FAB-12 (useSprints, useSprintDetails, etc.)
- Use existing UI components where possible
- Ensure responsive design

## Files to Create/Modify
- `src/routes/sprints/index.tsx` - sprint list page
- `src/routes/sprints/$sprintId.tsx` - sprint detail page
- `src/components/sprints/SprintCard.tsx`
- `src/components/sprints/SprintForm.tsx`
- `src/components/sprints/SprintStatusBadge.tsx`
- `src/components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` - add active sprint display

title: Task Assignment & Queue System
description:
## Overview
Implement a comprehensive task assignment and queue management system for sprint tasks.

## Scope
- Create MSW handlers for task operations:
  - `GET /api/sprints/:sprintId/tasks` - list tasks for a sprint
  - `POST /api/sprints/:sprintId/tasks` - create new task
  - `GET /api/tasks/:taskId` - get task details
  - `PATCH /api/tasks/:taskId/assign` - assign task to agent
  - `PATCH /api/tasks/:taskId/status` - update task status
  - `PATCH /api/tasks/:taskId` - update task fields
- Implement TanStack Query hooks:
  - `useSprintTasks(sprintId)` - fetch tasks for sprint
  - `useTaskDetails(taskId)` - fetch single task
  - `useAssignTask()` - mutation for assignment
  - `useUpdateTaskStatus()` - mutation for status changes
  - `useBulkTaskActions()` - mutation for bulk operations
- Create TypeScript types:
  - `Task` interface: id, title, description, assignee, status, priority, estimatedPoints, sprintId
  - `TaskStatus` enum: `BACKLOG | TODO | IN_PROGRESS | REVIEW | DONE`
  - `TaskPriority` enum: `LOW | MEDIUM | HIGH | CRITICAL`
- Build TanStack Table integration:
  - Configure table with columns: ID, title, assignee, status, priority, points
  - Implement sorting, filtering by status/assignee
  - Row selection for bulk actions
  - Inline editing for assignee (dropdown cell)
- Create task board route: `/sprints/:sprintId/tasks`
- Mock data factory for realistic task data

## Technical Notes
- Use TanStack Table v8 for table state and operations
- Follow existing patterns for MSW handlers and queries
- Ensure optimistic updates for smooth UX
- Support drag-drop in future (keep architecture flexible)
- Status transitions should be validated on backend

## Files to Create/Modify
- `src/mocks/handlers.ts` - add task endpoints
- `src/mocks/data/tasks.ts` - mock task data factory
- `src/hooks/useTasks.ts` - task query hooks
- `src/types/task.ts` - task type definitions
- `src/routes/sprints/$sprintId/tasks.tsx` - task board route
- `src/components/tasks/TaskTable.tsx` - TanStack Table component
- `src/components/tasks/AssigneeCell.tsx` - inline assignee editor

title: Agent Performance Analytics Dashboard
description:
## Overview
Create a comprehensive analytics dashboard to visualize agent performance metrics and task history.

## Scope
- Create agent analytics page (`/agents/:agentId/analytics`):
  - Performance metric cards:
    - Tasks completed (count)
    - Average cycle time (hours/days)
    - Error rate (percentage)
    - Average review comments per task
  - Time period selector: 24h / 7d / 30d
  - Simple trend indicators (up/down/stable)
- Build virtualized task history list using TanStack Virtual:
  - Display recent tasks with: title, status badge, completion date, cycle time
  - Support pagination/infinite scroll
  - Smooth scrolling for 100+ items
  - Click to view task details
- Create visualization components:
  - Use HTML Canvas for simple trend chart (line chart showing tasks completed over time)
  - Responsive card layout for metrics
  - Color-coded status badges
- Create reusable components:
  - `MetricCard` - displays single metric with value, label, trend
  - `TaskHistoryItem` - single task row in virtualized list
  - `AgentAnalyticsHeader` - agent info + period selector
  - `TrendChart` - simple canvas-based chart component

## Technical Notes
- Integrate with hooks from backend (useAgentMetrics, useAgentTaskHistory)
- Use TanStack Virtual for performant list rendering
- Keep chart implementation simple (Canvas API, no external chart library for MVP)
- Follow existing design system and component patterns
- Ensure responsive layout for different screen sizes
- Handle loading and error states gracefully

## Dependencies
- Requires agent and task data structures to be in place
- Will integrate with existing useAgentWorkload hook patterns

## Files to Create/Modify
- `src/routes/agents/$agentId/analytics.tsx` - analytics page route
- `src/components/analytics/MetricCard.tsx`
- `src/components/analytics/TaskHistoryList.tsx` - virtualized list
- `src/components/analytics/TaskHistoryItem.tsx`
- `src/components/analytics/TrendChart.tsx` - canvas chart
- `src/components/analytics/AgentAnalyticsHeader.tsx`
